hypothesis: If an EfficientNet-B0 model is pretrained on the data-rich additive-free SEM images and then fine-tuned on the small additive-induced SEM dataset, it will achieve higher class-wise accuracy and F1 on the nine-class NCM composition/aging classification task than models trained without source pretraining or from scratch, and fine-tuning with targeted oversampling will yield better class-wise performance than fine-tuning using weighted cross-entropy without oversampling.
